question_title: Will NVIDIA (NVDA) close above $225 on any trading day from July 20 to July 24, 2026?
summary: Stock threshold question: NVDA needs an official close above $225 on at least one day July 20-24, 2026, a ~6.7% move from the $210.96 July 10 close. A Monte Carlo simulation using empirical NVDA volatility (2.2-3.2%/day) and mild positive post-spike drift, requiring the max close over the 5 window days to exceed $225, yields ~0.33 and is robust across parameter variants (0.28-0.36). Favorable momentum (recent +8% run) is balanced against status-quo persistence and the absence of a scheduled binary catalyst in the window. Final estimate: 0.33.

factors.description: Monte Carlo from empirical NVDA volatility and starting price $210.96, requiring max close >$225 across the 5 window days (~6.7% above current). Weighted blend across vol/drift scenarios gives ~0.33, stable across variants (0.28-0.36)
Strong recent momentum (5d +8.3%, 10d +7.8%) plus shock-alert base rate: after +4% spikes, median 10d forward return +3.3%, 59% positive — mild upward tilt already reflected in drift assumption
$225 is reachable — stock closed $235 on May 14 and ~$212 mid-June; not an extreme move for this volatility, and only one of five days must cross
No hard binary catalyst in the window (Q2 earnings late Aug; H200/Rubin news diffuse with nothing scheduled July 20-24)

reasoning:
**Forecast: 33% (NO more likely than YES)**

NVDA would need to close above $225 on at least one day from July 20-24, 2026 — a ~6.7% gain from the July 10 close of $210.96. A Monte Carlo simulation using empirical volatility (~2.2-3.2%/day) and a mild positive post-spike drift, checking whether the maximum close across the 5 window days exceeds the threshold, yields **~0.33**, stable across parameter variants (0.28-0.36).

Key considerations:
- **For YES:** Strong recent momentum (+8% over 5 days, +4% on July 10), and the "only one of five days needs to cross" structure. $225 is well within recent range (stock was at $235 in mid-May).
- **For NO (status quo):** Requires a clean +6.7% break to a level not seen in ~5 weeks; 20-day realized vol is only 2.22%/day; no scheduled hard catalyst (earnings are late August) falls in the window.

Premortem review approved the forecast as well-grounded.
sources_consulted: [NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Stock Historical Prices & Data - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/history/)
[NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Stock Price, News, Quote & History - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if the official Nasdaq closing price of NVIDIA Corporation (ticker NVDA) common stock exceeds $225.00 on at least one U.S. equity trading day from July 20, 2026 through July 24, 2026, inclusive. It resolves NO otherwise. Resolution uses the daily "Close" value shown in the historical price table at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/history/ , or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) if Yahoo Finance is unavailable. For example, a close of $226.50 on any of those days resolves YES; if the highest close across all five days is $220.00, it resolves NO.
fine_print: For reference, NVDA closed at $210.96 on July 10, 2026, and its all-time closing high is $235.47 (May 14, 2026). Any stock split or reverse split affecting the share price during the window will be handled using the split-adjusted "Close" reported by the resolution source. Intraday highs that do not persist to the official daily close do not count.

factors.description: Monte Carlo from empirical NVDA volatility (~2.2-3.2%/day) and starting price $210.96, requiring max close >$225 across the 5 window days (July 20-24, ~6.7% above current). Weighted blend gives ~0.33
Strong recent momentum (5d +8.3%, 10d +7.8%) and shock-alert base rate: after +4% spikes, median 10d forward +3.3%, 59% positive — mild upward tilt already in drift
$225 is well within reach — stock closed $235 on May 14 and traded ~$212 mid-June; not an extreme move for this volatility
No major earnings catalyst in window (Q2 earnings late Aug); H200/Rubin news is diffuse, no scheduled binary catalyst July 20-24

input.anchor: Random-walk / empirical MC base rate: ~0.33 probability of max close exceeding threshold given volatility and 6.7% required move over ~2 weeks
input.assessment: Stock threshold question. Need NVDA official close >$225 on at least one day July 20-24. From July 10 close of $210.96, that's +6.7%. Monte Carlo across empirical volatility (2.2-3.2% daily) and mild positive drift (justified by post-spike base rate) yields ~0.33, stable across parameter variants (0.28-0.36). The 'max over 5 window days' structure helps YES since only one day needs to cross. Momentum is favorable but status quo (staying below $225) is the more likely outcome. No hard binary catalyst in the window.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor is the MC output itself. Momentum factor mildly raises, lack of catalyst mildly lowers. Not hedging to 50% — data supports ~0.33.
input.key_uncertainties: True forward volatility and drift after the spike; whether momentum continues or mean-reverts. A macro AI-sector shock could swing either way.
input.tool_audit: web_search returned rich structured NVDA data (20d vol 2.22%, trailing returns, shock alert base rates) — very useful. Sandbox MC ran cleanly. Polymarket reference was dated (stock at $195 then). No tool failures.
input.process_reflection: Smooth for a stock threshold question. The structured summary_stats (trailing vol, shock alerts with forward-return base rates) embedded in web_search were directly usable for simulation parameters — excellent. No friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all four factors against the research trace; the forecast is well-grounded and the probability is well-supported.

FACTOR VERIFICATION:
- Factor 1 (MC ~0.33): Trace shows two Monte Carlo runs. The final empirical-blend run (vol scenarios 2.2%/2.5%/2.8%/3.2%, drifts 0.0005-0.0015, weighted) returns 0.3325 (duration_ms 220, exit_code 0). Starting price $210.96 and threshold $225 (+6.66%) are correct. Grounded.
- Factor 2 (momentum + shock base rate): Trace summary_stats confirm 5-day +8.28%, 10-day +7.78%; shock_alert confirms median forward +3.3%, 59% positive, 61 comparable events after the +4.03% July 10 spike. Logit sign (+0.3, upward) matches description. Grounded.
- Factor 3 ($225 within reach): May 14 $235 all-time close is from question fine print; mid-June ~$212 matches trace June 15 close of $212.45. Logit +0.2 direction correct. Grounded.
- Factor 4 (no catalyst in window): Trace shows abundant but diffuse Rubin/H200 coverage with no scheduled binary event dated July 20-24. Logit -0.2 defensible. Grounded.

CONSISTENCY CHECKS:
- Anchor (0.33) equals final (0.33): no divergence to justify.
- No worldview NVDA price forecast contradicts this; only archived, off-topic NVDA entries (Korea visit, HBM qualification) exist.
- No double-counting: the agent explicitly noted momentum/reachability are baked into the MC drift and deliberately trusted the MC output (0.33) over the factor-additive implied 0.40 to avoid double-counting — good discipline.
- No pre-publication event: window (July 20-24) is entirely after published_at (July 13).
- Drift assumption is a legitimate empirical conditional base rate (post-spike forward returns), not regime-spanning contamination; and the zero-drift sensitivity rows still yield ~0.28-0.33, so the forecast does not hinge on it.
- Tool audit: agent claims 'no tool failures'; ground truth is 0 errors. Consistent.

INDEPENDENT PROBABILITY: With 20d realized vol of only 2.22%/day, a required +6.66% move to a single close among 5 days ~6-10 trading days out, and the max-over-5-days structure plus mild justified positive drift, my own estimate lands in 0.28-0.35. The agent's 0.33 sits squarely in that range. The stale Polymarket reference (32% above $216 when stock was ~$195) argues for caution but was correctly discounted as pre-momentum-run.

COUNTERARGUMENT/CONFIDENCE: The self-examination constructs a genuine two-sided alternative (0.40+ momentum-continuation vs 0.25 mean-reversion) with specific evidence and concrete mind-changers — a real attempt, not a token gesture. Confidence 0.80 is calibrated to the stable-but-path-uncertain MC.

No fabricated claims, no wrong-direction factors, no double-counting, no resolution misalignment. Approve.
